From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Canadian film festival

The **Fantasia International Film Festival**, also known as **Fantasia Fest** or simply **Fantasia,** is an annual [film festival](/wiki/Film_festival "Film festival") founded in 1996 and held primarily in [Montreal](/wiki/Montreal "Montreal"), Canada. It specializes in genre films, including [fantasy](/wiki/Fantasy_film "Fantasy film"), [horror](/wiki/Horror_film "Horror film"), [science fiction](/wiki/Science_fiction_film "Science fiction film"), and [cult](/wiki/Cult_film "Cult film") cinema, and draws over 100,000 attendees each year.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-scran2016-2)

*Fantasia* is frequently cited as the leading genre film festival in [North America](/wiki/North_America "North America"), and is considered one of the top three genre festivals worldwide, alongside [Sitges](/wiki/Sitges_Film_Festival "Sitges Film Festival") and [Fantastic Fest](/wiki/Fantastic_Fest "Fantastic Fest").[[3]](#cite_note-lang2021-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7) Its mission is to promote genre, anti-Hollywood cinema and assist independent filmmakers, having launched the careers of many modern [auteurs](/wiki/Auteur "Auteur") throughout the years.

Since 2012, the festival has also held the *Frontières* cinema market that allows promising projects to find potential producers and distributors. Since 2016, *Frontières* has partnered with the [Marché du Film](/wiki/March%C3%A9_du_Film "Marché du Film"), led by the [Cannes Film Festival](/wiki/Cannes_Film_Festival "Cannes Film Festival"),[[8]](#cite_note-8) and from 2026, with the [European Film Market](/wiki/European_Film_Market "European Film Market") at the [Berlin International Film Festival](/wiki/Berlin_International_Film_Festival "Berlin International Film Festival").[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## Overview

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fantasia_International_Film_Festival&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Overview")]

*Fantasia* focuses on niche, low-budget films of various genres, from horror to science fiction, most of them [non-PG](/wiki/Motion_Picture_Association_film_rating_system "Motion Picture Association film rating system"). It grew from the Asian Film scene in Montreal and still has a significant shift to Asian cinema.[[10]](#cite_note-bmovies-10)

*Fantasia* flagship section is named *Cheval Noir*, the winners are awarded with the festival's mascot statuette, a mighty black [pegasus](/wiki/Pegasus "Pegasus").[[11]](#cite_note-mack-11) Apart from *Cheval Noir*, the festival includes *New Flesh*, *Séquences*, *Camera Lucida*, *[Satoshi Kon](/wiki/Satoshi_Kon "Satoshi Kon")* prize, and several other sections, while in the wrap of the event the audience. Every year, the program offers master-classes and special screenings, as well as world and American premieres, press-conferences and meetings with actors and directors.[[12]](#cite_note-donato-12)[[13]](#cite_note-skinner-13)[[3]](#cite_note-lang2021-3) In 2016, a new section *Documentaries From the Edge* was established.[[14]](#cite_note-variety25-14)

Since 1997, Mitch Davis has been the festival's creative director. According to Davis, *Fantasia's* mission is to open niche, genre, noncommercial movies from independent auteurs to the audience, and give them deserved attention.[[14]](#cite_note-variety25-14) [Guillermo del Toro](/wiki/Guillermo_del_Toro "Guillermo del Toro") calls *Fantasia* a shrine to all geeks and a feast for those who love things completely unlovable to the most.[[15]](#cite_note-15) [Bill Plympton](/wiki/Bill_Plympton "Bill Plympton") treasures its anti-Hollywood atmosphere.[[16]](#cite_note-16) The audience at *Fantasia* is considered to be open-minded people with a good sense of humor and good, albeit somewhat unconventional, taste. Festival regulars say that audiences always meow loudly before the screening of feature films, a tradition that is still alive today.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

The fest has only two indoor locations, lots of events are held in usual cafes, restaurants, and karaoke-bars. The atmosphere is famous for its informal vibe, the screenings are open, no VIP zones are secluded and even the world famous directors and actors freely blend in the crowd.[[14]](#cite_note-variety25-14)[[18]](#cite_note-boisse-18)[[19]](#cite_note-filmmake-19) By 2020, *Fantasia* had already been recognized as one of the most important cinema fests in North America. Founded by cinephiles and initially based on sheer enthusiasm, it was supported only with private money for more than a decade. Only when its audience surpassed that of [WWF](/wiki/Montreal_World_Film_Festival "Montreal World Film Festival") and [FNC](/wiki/Festival_du_nouveau_cin%C3%A9ma "Festival du nouveau cinéma"), it attracted governmental attention and further funding.[[20]](#cite_note-20)[[21]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEGrondin1999-21)

*New Flesh* section is now considered "one of the world's premier launching pads for new voices in genre cinema".[[3]](#cite_note-lang2021-3)[[22]](#cite_note-22)

In 2012, *Fantasia* founded *Frontières* platform to promote genre films and help promising projects. For its first season, 12 projects were selected and then presented to producers and distributors. In 2013, *Frontières* was invited to host a European section at the [Brussels International Fantastic Film Festival](/wiki/Brussels_International_Fantastic_Film_Festival "Brussels International Fantastic Film Festival").[[23]](#cite_note-brendan2011-23)[[24]](#cite_note-hopewell16-24) In 2016, *Frontières* announced joint program with the [Cannes](/wiki/Cannes_Film_Festival "Cannes Film Festival")'s [Marché du Film](/wiki/March%C3%A9_du_Film "Marché du Film"): together they are to select and present promising projects to potential partners. *Frontières* was also offered to host its section of genre cinema at the [Cannes Film Festival](/wiki/Cannes_Film_Festival "Cannes Film Festival").[[24]](#cite_note-hopewell16-24)[[25]](#cite_note-25)[[26]](#cite_note-26)[[27]](#cite_note-27)

In its more than two decades of history, Fantasia has made a significant contribution to Canadian film culture, built its audience and helped arthouse cinema emerge from the underground. In cinematic circles they talk about an outstanding *Fantasia Generation* of creators, who grew with the festival. For example, [Roadkill Superstars](/wiki/RKSS "RKSS") were among the very first visitors. They even confess to forging documents to attend non-PG movies. Their *[Turbo Kid](/wiki/Turbo_Kid "Turbo Kid")* was funded and produced thanks to the *Frontières*.[[28]](#cite_note-sandwell2016-28)[[29]](#cite_note-kohn-29) Similarly, *Fantasia* and the *Frontières* helped [Astron-6](/wiki/Astron-6 "Astron-6") and financed *[The Void](/wiki/The_Void_(2016_film) "The Void (2016 film)")*, *[Radius](/wiki/Radius_(film) "Radius (film)")*, *[The Ranger](/wiki/The_Ranger_(film) "The Ranger (film)")*, and many more.[[30]](#cite_note-30)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fantasia_International_Film_Festival&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

### 1996–2005

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fantasia_International_Film_Festival&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: 1996–2005")]

*Fant-Asia* 1996 was launched by "three Hong Kong new wave fanboys" Martin Sauvageau, André Dubois and Pierre Corbeil, who felt a growing local interest in Asian films and the desire to watch them on the big screen. The 1996 edition of the festival lasted for a whole month and featured six months worth of entirely Asian fantasy and action movies with a significant prevalence of [Chow Yun-fat](/wiki/Chow_Yun-fat "Chow Yun-fat") and [Jet Li](/wiki/Jet_Li "Jet Li") retrospectives.[[31]](#cite_note-31)[[13]](#cite_note-skinner-13)[[32]](#cite_note-32)[[33]](#cite_note-33)

FantAsia 1997 was held from July 11 to August 10. The lineup included *[Perfect Blue](/wiki/Perfect_Blue "Perfect Blue")*, *[Drunken Master II](/wiki/Drunken_Master_II "Drunken Master II")*, *[Stagefright](/wiki/Stage_Fright_(1987_film) "Stage Fright (1987 film)")*, *[El Dia de la Bestia](/wiki/The_Day_of_the_Beast_(film) "The Day of the Beast (film)")*, *Henry*, *[Tromeo and Juliet](/wiki/Tromeo_and_Juliet "Tromeo and Juliet")*, *[Cutting Moments](/wiki/Cutting_Moments "Cutting Moments")* and others. *[A Gun for Jennifer](/wiki/A_Gun_for_Jennifer "A Gun for Jennifer")* won the main prize and was met by a standing ovation of the audience.[[34]](#cite_note-34)

In 1998, Fantasia's [Toronto](/wiki/Toronto "Toronto") edition premiered at the [Bloor Cinema](/wiki/Bloor_Cinema "Bloor Cinema"). That year, [Tony Timpone](/wiki/Tony_Timpone "Tony Timpone") joined the management team.[[19]](#cite_note-filmmake-19)[[35]](#cite_note-35)

2002 became the missed year for Fantasia due to problems with the main venue, Imperial Cinema. Only four months before the event, the theater announced cancellation because of the broken air conditioning system. Indeed, the theater was not repaired until 2004. Alternate bookings were not available. The lack of assurance for the 2003 festival meant a change in venue and so, Fantasia 2003 moved [Concordia University](/wiki/Concordia_University_(Quebec) "Concordia University (Quebec)").[[36]](#cite_note-visitorsguide-36)

Fantasia 2003 was held for the first time on the [Concordia University](/wiki/Concordia_University_(Quebec) "Concordia University (Quebec)") campus,[[36]](#cite_note-visitorsguide-36) using the *de Sève Cinema* and *Henry F. Hall Alumni Auditorium*. This was also the first year that a DVD filled with movie trailers of movies shown at the festival was available for purchase with the festival guide book.

### 2006

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fantasia_International_Film_Festival&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 2006")]

Fantasia's 10th anniversary and 10th edition, the 2006 edition, is the first to feature free outdoor shows. The outdoors shows are at [Parc de la Paix](/w/index.php?title=Parc_de_la_Paix&action=edit&redlink=1 "Parc de la Paix (page does not exist)") and are free. Outdoor projections included films from the previous editions: *[Kamikaze Girls](/wiki/Kamikaze_Girls "Kamikaze Girls")*, the last four episodes of *[Goldorak](/wiki/Goldorak "Goldorak")*, *[Night of the Living Dorks](/wiki/Night_of_the_Living_Dorks "Night of the Living Dorks")* and *[Attack the Gas Station](/wiki/Attack_the_Gas_Station "Attack the Gas Station")*. The indoor shows use the same [Concordia University](/wiki/Concordia_University_(Quebec) "Concordia University (Quebec)") facilities as since the move to Concordia. The outdoor shows are several kilometres away from the indoor shows. The main prize was won by *[Blood Curse](/wiki/Blood_Curse_(film) "Blood Curse (film)")*. With its 10th anniversary, Fantasia helped to launch an associated but separate Toronto festival [Toronto After Dark Film Festival](/wiki/Toronto_After_Dark_Film_Festival "Toronto After Dark Film Festival").[[37]](#cite_note-jordan2006-37)

### 2007

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fantasia_International_Film_Festival&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2007")]

This edition was held from July 5 to July 23 at the [Concordia University](/wiki/Concordia_University_(Quebec) "Concordia University (Quebec)"). In addition to the Hall hall and DeSeve hall, a third screening room has been added at the *D.B. Clarke Theatre*. There were, however, no outdoor shows this year. Montreal film *[Flutter](/wiki/Flutter_(2006_film) "Flutter (2006 film)")* received the award for best Quebec short feature.[[38]](#cite_note-38) *[Memories of Matsuko](/wiki/Memories_of_Matsuko "Memories of Matsuko")* was selected as the best Asian feature film, in European program won the *[Hatchet](/wiki/Hatchet_(film) "Hatchet (film)")*. In animation the main prize went to *[We Are the Strange](/wiki/We_Are_the_Strange "We Are the Strange")*. A total of more than 81,000 tickets were sold for Fantasia 2007. Mitch Davis signed a first-look production deal with [Paramount](/wiki/Paramount_Pictures "Paramount Pictures")'s Blumhouse Prods. to scout for new projects and foreign movies for potential remakes.[[39]](#cite_note-variety2007-39)

### 2008

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fantasia_International_Film_Festival&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: 2008")]

In 2008, Fantasia was held from July 3 to July 21. It featured the world premieres of *[Pig Hunt](/wiki/Pig_Hunt "Pig Hunt")*, *[Home Movie](/wiki/Home_Movie_(2008_film) "Home Movie (2008 film)")*, *[Repo! The Genetic Opera](/wiki/Repo!_The_Genetic_Opera "Repo! The Genetic Opera")*, *[Midnight Meat Train](/wiki/Midnight_Meat_Train "Midnight Meat Train")*, Eric Shapiro's *Rule of Three*, *[Truffles](/wiki/Truffles_(film) "Truffles (film)") (Truffe)*, *[Treevenge](/wiki/Treevenge "Treevenge")*, *[The Facts in the Case of Mister Hollow](/wiki/The_Facts_in_the_Case_of_Mister_Hollow "The Facts in the Case of Mister Hollow")*, *Electric Fence*, *Paradox Mary*, *Laura Panic*, and *Don't Worry*.[[40]](#cite_note-40) [Gordon Liu](/wiki/Gordon_Liu "Gordon Liu") attended the screening of *[Disciples of the 36th Chamber](/wiki/Disciples_of_the_36th_Chamber "Disciples of the 36th Chamber")* as a guest star.[[39]](#cite_note-variety2007-39)

### 2009

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fantasia_International_Film_Festival&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: 2009")]

In 2009, Fantasia was held from July 9 to July 29, opened with [Takashi Miike](/wiki/Takashi_Miike "Takashi Miike")'s film *[Yatterman](/wiki/Yatterman_(film) "Yatterman (film)")* and ended on July 29, 2009 with the North American premiere of [Quentin Tarantino](/wiki/Quentin_Tarantino "Quentin Tarantino")'s film *[Inglourious Basterds](/wiki/Inglourious_Basterds "Inglourious Basterds")*.[[41]](#cite_note-41)[[42]](#cite_note-42) Among the films screened at Fantasia 2009 were *[Thirst](/wiki/Thirst_(2009_film) "Thirst (2009 film)")*, *[Love Exposure](/wiki/Love_Exposure "Love Exposure")*, *[Dream](/wiki/Dream_(2008_film) "Dream (2008 film)")*, *[Embodiment of Evil](/wiki/Embodiment_of_Evil "Embodiment of Evil")*, *[Vampire Girl vs. Frankenstein Girl](/wiki/Vampire_Girl_vs._Frankenstein_Girl "Vampire Girl vs. Frankenstein Girl")*, *[Smash Cut](/wiki/Smash_Cut "Smash Cut")*, *[Trick 'r Treat](/wiki/Trick_%27r_Treat "Trick 'r Treat")*, *[Neighbor](/wiki/Neighbor_(2009_film) "Neighbor (2009 film)")*, *Must Love Death*, *[Cencoroll](/wiki/Cencoroll "Cencoroll")*, *[Dread](/wiki/Dread_(film) "Dread (film)")*.[[43]](#cite_note-43)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/0/05/FantasiaFestival_50x70.jpg/250px-FantasiaFestival_50x70.jpg)](/wiki/File:FantasiaFestival_50x70.jpg)

### 2010s

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fantasia_International_Film_Festival&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: 2010s")]

#### 2010

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fantasia_International_Film_Festival&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: 2010")]

The films for Fantasia 2010 were announced on Tuesday, June 29, 2010. Tickets went on sale on July 6, 2010 at 1 pm. The Festival started on July 8, 2010 running until July 28, 2010 with 6 indoor screening venues and one outdoor location. For 2010, a permanent blog was introduced to communicate with fans year-round. Opening film: [The Sorcerer's Apprentice](/wiki/The_Sorcerer%27s_Apprentice_(2010_film) "The Sorcerer's Apprentice (2010 film)"), presented by [Jay Baruchel](/wiki/Jay_Baruchel "Jay Baruchel"). Among Canadian premieres were *[Scott Pilgrim vs. the World](/wiki/Scott_Pilgrim_vs._the_World "Scott Pilgrim vs. the World")*, *[The Last Exorcism](/wiki/The_Last_Exorcism "The Last Exorcism")*, *[The Violent Kind](/wiki/The_Violent_Kind "The Violent Kind")*, *[Van Von Hunter](/wiki/Van_Von_Hunter "Van Von Hunter")*, *[Black Lightning](/wiki/Black_Lightning_(2009_film) "Black Lightning (2009 film)")*. World premieres included *[I Spit on Your Grave](/wiki/I_Spit_on_Your_Grave_(2010_film) "I Spit on Your Grave (2010 film)")*, *[The Shrine](/wiki/The_Shrine_(film) "The Shrine (film)")*, etc.[[44]](#cite_note-44) The lineup included *[A Serbian Film](/wiki/A_Serbian_Film "A Serbian Film")*, *[Air Doll](/wiki/Air_Doll "Air Doll")*, *[Best Worst Movie](/wiki/Best_Worst_Movie "Best Worst Movie")*, *[The House of the Devil](/wiki/The_House_of_the_Devil_(2009_film) "The House of the Devil (2009 film)")*, *[Smash Cut](/wiki/Smash_Cut "Smash Cut")*, and many more. The special screening of *[Combat Shock](/wiki/Combat_Shock "Combat Shock")* was attended by [Buddy Giovinazzo](/wiki/Buddy_Giovinazzo "Buddy Giovinazzo").[[45]](#cite_note-45)[[46]](#cite_note-46) Pater Sparrow's *1* received the Cheval Noir as best feature film of the year.[[47]](#cite_note-47)[[48]](#cite_note-48)

#### 2011

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fantasia_International_Film_Festival&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: 2011")]

Fantasia 2011 held 19 world premieres and a lineup of 134 movies from 25 countries.[[49]](#cite_note-barton-49) The festival opened with the Canadian Premiere of *[Red State](/wiki/Red_State_(2011_film) "Red State (2011 film)")*, other premieres included *[Final Destination 5](/wiki/Final_Destination_5 "Final Destination 5")*. Also notable was the world premiere of the Swedish [horror film](/wiki/Horror_film "Horror film") *[Marianne](/wiki/Marianne_(2011_film) "Marianne (2011 film)").*[[50]](#cite_note-50)[[51]](#cite_note-51)

[Yoshimasa Ishibashi](/wiki/Yoshimasa_Ishibashi "Yoshimasa Ishibashi") was awarded with the Best Director prize (for *Milocrorze: A love story*). Best Screenplay award was given to [Park Hoon-jung](/wiki/Park_Hoon-jung "Park Hoon-jung") (for *[The Unjust](/wiki/The_Unjust "The Unjust")*). *I Love You* short from *[The Theatre Bizarre](/wiki/The_Theatre_Bizarre "The Theatre Bizarre")* was specially noted by the jury. Best Animation prize went to *[Surviving Life](/wiki/Surviving_Life "Surviving Life")*.[[49]](#cite_note-barton-49) [Amiel Courtin-Wilson](/wiki/Amiel_Courtin-Wilson "Amiel Courtin-Wilson")'s *[Hail](/w/index.php?title=Hail_(2011)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Hail (2011) (page does not exist)")* won in *The Séquences*.[[12]](#cite_note-donato-12)

The festival also featured the presentation to [John Landis](/wiki/John_Landis "John Landis") of a lifetime Achievement award, while Landis presented his new film *[Burke and Hare](/wiki/Burke_and_Hare_(2010_film) "Burke and Hare (2010 film)")*.[[52]](#cite_note-52)

#### 2012

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fantasia_International_Film_Festival&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: 2012")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/df/Montreal_050.jpg/250px-Montreal_050.jpg)](/wiki/File:Montreal_050.jpg)

Premier of *[Alter Egos](/wiki/Alter_Egos "Alter Egos")* at Fantasia Film Festival in Montreal July 24, 2012

In 2012, Fantasia was held from 19 July to 7 August.[[23]](#cite_note-brendan2011-23) Its 90,000 audience exceeded [WFF](/wiki/Montreal_World_Film_Festival "Montreal World Film Festival")'s, the festival was extensively covered in the world media.[[53]](#cite_note-var2014-53)[[54]](#cite_note-54)

Fantasia 2012 featured the screenings of the films *[Toad Road](/wiki/Toad_Road "Toad Road")*, *[Doomsday Book](/wiki/Doomsday_Book_(film) "Doomsday Book (film)")* (won the main prize), the horror anthology film *[V/H/S](/wiki/V/H/S "V/H/S")*, *[Hidden in the Woods (original version)](/wiki/Hidden_in_the_Woods_(original_version) "Hidden in the Woods (original version)")*, *[Seediq Bale](/wiki/Seediq_Bale "Seediq Bale")*, and [Quentin Dupieux](/wiki/Quentin_Dupieux "Quentin Dupieux")'s *[Wrong](/wiki/Wrong_(film) "Wrong (film)")*.[[55]](#cite_note-55)[[56]](#cite_note-fantasia-56)

#### 2013

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fantasia_International_Film_Festival&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: 2013")]

In 2013, Fantasia was held from 18 July to 7 August, its program included 120 films. Among premieres were *[Antosocial](/wiki/Antisocial_(film) "Antisocial (film)")* and *[Curse of Chucky](/wiki/Curse_of_Chucky "Curse of Chucky")*. The lineup featured *[Return to Nuke 'Em High Volume 1](/wiki/Return_to_Nuke_%27Em_High_Volume_1 "Return to Nuke 'Em High Volume 1")*, *[Raze](/wiki/Raze_(film) "Raze (film)")*, *[Bad Milo!](/wiki/Bad_Milo! "Bad Milo!")*, *[Willow Creek (film)](/wiki/Willow_Creek_(film) "Willow Creek (film)")*,[[57]](#cite_note-57)[[58]](#cite_note-58) *[After School Midnighters](/wiki/After_School_Midnighters "After School Midnighters")*, *[I'll Give It My All... Tomorrow](/wiki/I%27ll_Give_It_My_All..._Tomorrow "I'll Give It My All... Tomorrow")*, *[The Complex](/wiki/The_Complex_(film) "The Complex (film)")*, *[Gatchaman](/wiki/Gatchaman_(film) "Gatchaman (film)")*,[[59]](#cite_note-59) *[The World's End](/wiki/The_World%27s_End_(film) "The World's End (film)")*, and many more.[[60]](#cite_note-ewcom13-60)

*[Big Bad Wolves](/wiki/Big_Bad_Wolves "Big Bad Wolves")* took the main prize as the best feature film, [Hou Chi-jan](/wiki/Hou_Chi-jan "Hou Chi-jan") became the best director for his movie *[When a Wolf Falls in Love with a Sheep](/wiki/When_a_Wolf_Falls_in_Love_with_a_Sheep "When a Wolf Falls in Love with a Sheep")*.[[61]](#cite_note-61) *[Andrzej Żuławski](/wiki/Andrzej_%C5%BBu%C5%82awski "Andrzej Żuławski")* received the achievement award, while at the *New Flesh* section [Evan Louis Katz](/wiki/E._L._Katz "E. L. Katz") won with his debut *[Cheap Thrills](/wiki/Cheap_Thrills_(film) "Cheap Thrills (film)")*.[[60]](#cite_note-ewcom13-60)

#### 2014

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fantasia_International_Film_Festival&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: 2014")]

Fantasia 2014 lasted from July 17 to August 5, it featured 160 movies and had 216 screenings, while the audience grew to 129,000.[[53]](#cite_note-var2014-53) That year's poster was created by Donald Caron, it paid tribute to [Ray Harryhausen](/wiki/Ray_Harryhausen "Ray Harryhausen"), [Ray Bradbury](/wiki/Ray_Bradbury "Ray Bradbury"), [Forrest J Ackerman](/wiki/Forrest_J_Ackerman "Forrest J Ackerman") who flew across the sky in the *Bewitched Canoe* (a piece of Québec folklore, *[La Chasse Galerie](/wiki/Chasse-galerie "Chasse-galerie")* piloted by the Devil).[[62]](#cite_note-62) *[Jacky in Women's Kingdom](/wiki/Jacky_in_Women%27s_Kingdom "Jacky in Women's Kingdom")* became the opening film.[[63]](#cite_note-2014indie-63) Premieres included *[Unfriended](/wiki/Unfriended "Unfriended")* by [Levan Gabriadze](/wiki/Levan_Gabriadze "Levan Gabriadze"), *[The Midnight Swim](/wiki/The_Midnight_Swim "The Midnight Swim")* by [Sarah Adina Smith](/wiki/Sarah_Adina_Smith "Sarah Adina Smith"), *[The Creeping Garden](/wiki/The_Creeping_Garden "The Creeping Garden")*.[[53]](#cite_note-var2014-53)[[64]](#cite_note-Screen_Daily-64)

At the *New Flesh* section won Hugh Sullivan's *[The Infinite Man](/wiki/The_Infinite_Man_(film) "The Infinite Man (film)")*. [Mamoru Oshii](/wiki/Mamoru_Oshii "Mamoru Oshii") and [Tobe Hooper](/wiki/Tobe_Hooper "Tobe Hooper") were honoured with the Achievements award. *[Giovanni's Island](/wiki/Giovanni%27s_Island "Giovanni's Island")* by Mizuho Nishikubo won the main prize for best animation.[[53]](#cite_note-var2014-53)

#### 2015

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fantasia_International_Film_Festival&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: 2015")]

Fantasia-2015 had 22 world premieres, including *[Momentum](/wiki/Momentum_(2015_film) "Momentum (2015 film)")*. For the first time the festival introduced the VR-section. This year program featured *[Anguish](/wiki/Anguish_(2015_film) "Anguish (2015 film)")*, *[Wild City](/wiki/Wild_City "Wild City")*, *[Goodnight Mommy](/wiki/Goodnight_Mommy "Goodnight Mommy")*, and many more.[[19]](#cite_note-filmmake-19)[[65]](#cite_note-cott-65)

[Sion Sono](/wiki/Sion_Sono "Sion Sono") received the main prize for *[Tag](/wiki/Tag_(2015_film) "Tag (2015 film)")*, [Reina Triendl](/wiki/Reina_Triendl "Reina Triendl") became the best actress. *[Miss Hokusai](/wiki/Miss_Hokusai "Miss Hokusai")* won in Animation, *[La La La at Rock Bottom](/wiki/La_La_La_at_Rock_Bottom "La La La at Rock Bottom")* won in the Best Screenplay.[[65]](#cite_note-cott-65)

#### 2016

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fantasia_International_Film_Festival&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: 2016")]

For the first time in its history, Fantasia's audience exceeded 100,000. That year the festival had 209 screenings and 19 world premieres, including *[King Dave](/wiki/King_Dave "King Dave")* by [Daniel Grou](/wiki/Daniel_Grou "Daniel Grou"), *[Goran](/wiki/Goran_(film) "Goran (film)")* by [Nevio Marasović](/wiki/Nevio_Marasovi%C4%87 "Nevio Marasović"), *[Realive](/wiki/Realive "Realive")* by [Mateo Gil](/wiki/Mateo_Gil "Mateo Gil"), [Some Freaks](/wiki/Some_Freaks "Some Freaks"), etc.[[2]](#cite_note-scran2016-2)[[66]](#cite_note-66) [Yeon Sang-ho](/wiki/Yeon_Sang-ho "Yeon Sang-ho")'s *[Train to Busan](/wiki/Train_to_Busan "Train to Busan")* won as the best feature film, [Kiyoshi Kurosawa](/wiki/Kiyoshi_Kurosawa "Kiyoshi Kurosawa") became the best director (for *[Creepy](/wiki/Creepy_(film) "Creepy (film)")*). Award for the best screenplay went to [E J-yong](/wiki/E_J-yong "E J-yong") for *[The Bacchus Lady](/wiki/The_Bacchus_Lady "The Bacchus Lady")*. [Guillermo del Toro](/wiki/Guillermo_del_Toro "Guillermo del Toro") was honoured with Achievement award.[[13]](#cite_note-skinner-13)[[28]](#cite_note-sandwell2016-28)[[67]](#cite_note-67) Agnieszka Smoczyńska received the special mention of the jury for *[The Lure](/wiki/The_Lure_(2015_film) "The Lure (2015 film)")*.

#### 2017

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fantasia_International_Film_Festival&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: 2017")]

Fantasia-2017 went from July 13 to August 2 with a lineup of more than 150 feature films and 300 shorts,[[29]](#cite_note-kohn-29)[[68]](#cite_note-68) from [Luc Besson](/wiki/Luc_Besson "Luc Besson")'s $200 mln *[Valerian and the City of a Thousand Planets](/wiki/Valerian_and_the_City_of_a_Thousand_Planets "Valerian and the City of a Thousand Planets")* to [Nabwana I.G.G.](/wiki/Nabwana_I.G.G. "Nabwana I.G.G.")'s $65 *[Bad Black](/wiki/Bad_Black "Bad Black")*.[[69]](#cite_note-69) *[The Villainess](/wiki/The_Villainess "The Villainess")* opened the fest and the *[A Taxi Driver](/wiki/A_Taxi_Driver "A Taxi Driver")* closed. *[Atomic Blonde](/wiki/Atomic_Blonde "Atomic Blonde")*, [Agnieszka Holland](/wiki/Agnieszka_Holland "Agnieszka Holland")'s *[Spoor](/wiki/Spoor_(film) "Spoor (film)")* and *[You Only Live Once](/wiki/You_Only_Live_Once_(2017_film) "You Only Live Once (2017 film)")* premiered.[[70]](#cite_note-2017winners-70)[[71]](#cite_note-71)[[72]](#cite_note-72) [Robert Pattinson](/wiki/Robert_Pattinson "Robert Pattinson") presented *[Good Time](/wiki/Good_Time_(film) "Good Time (film)")*.[[73]](#cite_note-73) [Violetta Schurawlow](/wiki/Violetta_Schurawlow "Violetta Schurawlow") won the Best Actress for her role in *[Cold Hell](/wiki/Cold_Hell "Cold Hell")*, [Nattawut Poonpiriya](/wiki/Nattawut_Poonpiriya "Nattawut Poonpiriya") became the Best Director for his *[Bad Genius](/wiki/Bad_Genius_(2017_film) "Bad Genius (2017 film)")*.[[70]](#cite_note-2017winners-70)

#### 2018

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fantasia_International_Film_Festival&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: 2018")]

Fantasia-2018 was held from 12 July to 2 August and included 125 feature films and 220 shorts. That year poster featured a flying cat and a space pug.[[74]](#cite_note-74) The festival was opened by Santiago Menghini's short *[Milk](/wiki/Milk "Milk")* and [Daniel Roby](/wiki/Daniel_Roby "Daniel Roby")'s *[Just a Breath Away](/wiki/Just_a_Breath_Away "Just a Breath Away")*. The list of premieres included *[The Man Who Killed Hitler and Then the Bigfoot](/wiki/The_Man_Who_Killed_Hitler_and_Then_the_Bigfoot "The Man Who Killed Hitler and Then the Bigfoot")*, *[Mandy](/wiki/Mandy_(2018_film) "Mandy (2018 film)")*, [Kenji Tanigaki](/wiki/Kenji_Tanigaki "Kenji Tanigaki") *Big Brother*, [LaPlace's Witch](/wiki/Laplace%27s_Witch_(film) "Laplace's Witch (film)"), [Black Tide](/wiki/Black_Tide_(film) "Black Tide (film)"). [Joe Dante](/wiki/Joe_Dante "Joe Dante") was honoured with the Achievement award.[[75]](#cite_note-75)[[76]](#cite_note-76)[[18]](#cite_note-boisse-18)

*[Just a Breath Away](/wiki/Just_a_Breath_Away "Just a Breath Away")* won the main prize, however, some criticized that decision and mentioned that many other movies that year were better made, while in *[Just a Breath Away](/wiki/Just_a_Breath_Away "Just a Breath Away")* the leading cast lacked genuine emotions and delivered poorly. [Nosipho Dumisa](/wiki/Nosipho_Dumisa "Nosipho Dumisa") was awarded the Best Director prize for his *[Number 37](/wiki/Number_37_(film) "Number 37 (film)")*. *[Cam](/wiki/Cam_(film) "Cam (film)")* won in the *New Flesh* section, its creator [Isa Mazzei](/wiki/Isa_Mazzei "Isa Mazzei") also was awarded with *Cheval Noir Best Screenplay*. [Kim Da-mi](/wiki/Kim_Da-mi "Kim Da-mi") became the Best Actress for her role in *[The Witch: Part 1. The Subversion](/wiki/The_Witch:_Part_1._The_Subversion "The Witch: Part 1. The Subversion")*. *[Microhabitat](/wiki/Microhabitat_(film) "Microhabitat (film)")* won in *Camera Lucida* section.[[77]](#cite_note-77)[[78]](#cite_note-78)

#### 2019

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fantasia_International_Film_Festival&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: 2019")]

Fantasia 2019 lasted from July 11 to August 1.[[10]](#cite_note-bmovies-10) That year's poster was created by Donald Caron and depicted new favourites of the audience – a luchador pug and enormous flying cat.[[79]](#cite_note-79) The lineup of more than 100 feature films and numerous shorts[[80]](#cite_note-80) included *[Ready or Not](/wiki/Ready_or_Not_(2019_film) "Ready or Not (2019 film)")*, *[Sadako](/wiki/Sadako_(film) "Sadako (film)")*, *[The Lodge](/wiki/The_Lodge_(film) "The Lodge (film)")*, *[Vivarium](/wiki/Vivarium_(film) "Vivarium (film)")*, *[Killerman](/wiki/Killerman "Killerman")*, *[Master Z: Ip Man Legacy](/wiki/Master_Z:_Ip_Man_Legacy "Master Z: Ip Man Legacy")*, *[Satanic Panic](/wiki/Satanic_Panic_(film) "Satanic Panic (film)")*, and many more.[[81]](#cite_note-81) The festival held a 25th anniversary screening of *[The Crow](/wiki/The_Crow_(1994_film) "The Crow (1994 film)")*.[[82]](#cite_note-tvandnow-82)

*Best Asian Feature* went to *[Fly Me to the Saitama](/wiki/Fly_Me_to_the_Saitama "Fly Me to the Saitama")*, *Best International Feature* - to *[Lake Michigan Monster](/wiki/Lake_Michigan_Monster "Lake Michigan Monster")*, *Best Action! Feature* became *[The Gangster, the Cop, the Devil](/wiki/The_Gangster,_the_Cop,_the_Devil "The Gangster, the Cop, the Devil")*.[[83]](#cite_note-83) *Cheval Noir Best Feature* went to *[Idol](/wiki/Idol_(film) "Idol (film)")*, *Best Director* and *Best Screenplay* went to Carlo Mirabella-Davis for *[Swallow](/wiki/Swallow_(2019_film) "Swallow (2019 film)")*.[[84]](#cite_note-84) In *New Flesh* section won Kirill Sokolov's *[Why Don't You Just Die!](/wiki/Why_Don%27t_You_Just_Die! "Why Don't You Just Die!")*.[[85]](#cite_note-85) [Edward R. Pressman](/wiki/Edward_R._Pressman "Edward R. Pressman") received the *Achievement Award*.[[82]](#cite_note-tvandnow-82)

### 2020s

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fantasia_International_Film_Festival&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: 2020s")]

#### 2020

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fantasia_International_Film_Festival&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: 2020")]

In 2020, due to [COVID-19](/wiki/COVID-19 "COVID-19") restrictions, the festival went online and was moved to late summer, starting on 20 August and ending on 2 September. The screenings at Festival Scope and Shift72 were accessible from Canadian IP addresses. Among more than 100 feature films, selected for the festival, were [Neil Marshall](/wiki/Neil_Marshall "Neil Marshall")'s *[The Reckoning](/wiki/The_Reckoning_(2020_film) "The Reckoning (2020 film)")*, Shinichiro Ueda's *Special Actors*, [Nobuhiko Obayashi](/wiki/Nobuhiko_Obayashi "Nobuhiko Obayashi")'s *[Labyrinth of Cinema](/wiki/Labyrinth_of_Cinema "Labyrinth of Cinema")*, [Brea Grant](/wiki/Brea_Grant "Brea Grant")'s *[12 Hour Shift](/wiki/12_Hour_Shift "12 Hour Shift")*,[[86]](#cite_note-86)[[87]](#cite_note-87) *[Shakespeare's Shitstorm](/wiki/Shakespeare%27s_Shitstorm "Shakespeare's Shitstorm")*, *[A Costume for Nicholas](/wiki/A_Costume_for_Nicholas "A Costume for Nicholas")*, and many more.[[88]](#cite_note-88)

*Best Asian Feature* went to *Special Actors* by Shinichiro Ueda, *Best European/North-South American Feature* – to *[The Mortuary Collection](/wiki/The_Mortuary_Collection "The Mortuary Collection")*. *[Come True](/wiki/Come_True "Come True")* was honoured as the Most Groundbreaking film of the festival.[[89]](#cite_note-rue2020-89)

Closing film: *[The Great Yokai War: Guardians](/wiki/The_Great_Yokai_War:_Guardians "The Great Yokai War: Guardians")*.[[90]](#cite_note-90)

#### 2021

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fantasia_International_Film_Festival&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: 2021")]

The 25th anniversary edition featured 25 feature film world premieres, 12 international and 34 North American premieres. Opening film: *[Brain Freeze](/wiki/Brain_Freeze_(film) "Brain Freeze (film)")*.[[91]](#cite_note-screendaily2021-91)

*Cheval Noir Competition-Best Film Award* was given to *[Voice of Silence](/wiki/Voice_of_Silence_(2020_film) "Voice of Silence (2020 film)")* by [Hong Eui-jeong](/wiki/Hong_Eui-jeong "Hong Eui-jeong"), *Cheval Noir Competition-Best Actor Award* took [Yoo Ah-in](/wiki/Yoo_Ah-in "Yoo Ah-in"), Best Actress – Zelda Adams (for *[Hellbender](/wiki/Hellbender_(film) "Hellbender (film)")*). Best Screenplay award went to *[The Righteous](/wiki/The_Righteous_(2021_film) "The Righteous (2021 film)")*. *[Beyond the Infinite Two Minutes](/wiki/Beyond_the_Infinite_Two_Minutes "Beyond the Infinite Two Minutes")* won the Audience prize.[[91]](#cite_note-screendaily2021-91)

#### 2022

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fantasia_International_Film_Festival&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: 2022")]

The 26th edition of the festival was held from 14 July to 3 August 2022. In the festival over 130 features and 200 shorts were screened. #### 2024

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fantasia_International_Film_Festival&action=edit&section=24 "Edit section: 2024")]

The [28th edition of Fantasia](/wiki/28th_Fantasia_International_Film_Festival "28th Fantasia International Film Festival") was held July 18 – August 4.[[101]](#cite_note-101) The festival's opening film was *[Bookworm](/wiki/Bookworm_(film) "Bookworm (film)")*, by [Ant Timpson](/wiki/Ant_Timpson "Ant Timpson"),[[102]](#cite_note-102) and the festival closed with [André Forcier](/wiki/Andr%C3%A9_Forcier "André Forcier")'s film *[Ababooned](/wiki/Ababooned "Ababooned") (Ababouiné)*.[[103]](#cite_note-103) Forcier received the festival's Prix Denis-Héroux in honour of his overall career achievements.

A special Trailblazer Award was presented to Canadian filmmaker [Vincenzo Natali](/wiki/Vincenzo_Natali "Vincenzo Natali"), the director of the influential science fiction thriller film *[Cube](/wiki/Cube_(1997_film) "Cube (1997 film)")*, which also received a special screening of a new [4K](/wiki/4K_video "4K video") restoration on July 30, 2024.[[104]](#cite_note-104)

#### 2025

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fantasia_International_Film_Festival&action=edit&section=25 "Edit section: 2025")]

The [29th edition of Fantasia](/wiki/29th_Fantasia_International_Film_Festival "29th Fantasia International Film Festival") was held from July 17 to August 3.[[105]](#cite_note-105) The festival opened with American neo-Western black comedy film *[Eddington](/wiki/Eddington_(film) "Eddington (film)")*, by [Ari Aster](/wiki/Ari_Aster "Ari Aster"), and the Canadian Trailblazer Award was presented to Hungarian-born Canadian filmmaker [George Mihalka](/wiki/George_Mihalka "George Mihalka"), the director of the 1981 slasher film *[My Bloody Valentine](/wiki/My_Bloody_Valentine_(film) "My Bloody Valentine (film)")*.[[106]](#cite_note-106) Announced on July 26, the jury awarded prizes in seven of the competition’s eight categories, with the Cheval Noir Award for Best Film going to *Mother of Flies*, a horror–fantasy film by John Adams, Zelda Adams, and Toby Posner. This marks the first time a U.S. production has received the prestigious award.[[107]](#cite_note-107)
